bbc.title: No need to panic for England - but this was a reality check
bbc.desc: England's drab goalless draw with Ghana is no cause to panic - but it does serve as a reality check, writes Phil McNulty.

bbc.title: Watch: Football fans celebrate in Ghana after draw with England
bbc.desc: Ghana correspondent Thomas Naadi reports from Black Star Square in Accra.

bbc.title: Hundreds of schools plan closures ahead of red heat alerts
bbc.desc: The temperature topped 34.6 C in Wisley, England, on Tuesday while Scotland and Northern Ireland saw their hottest days of the year.

bbc.title: The UK's summers are getting hotter - but how prepared are we?
bbc.desc: Based on current trends parts of the UK are set to see 40C summers regularly within a couple of decades.

bbc.title: Starmer holds talks with Burnham as he seeks 'orderly' transition
bbc.desc: The outgoing PM is offering "access talks" with the civil service to Labour leadership candidates.

bbc.title: Ten years on, Brexit's economic impact is becoming clearer
bbc.desc: A decade ago, many economists argued the UK would sustain longer-term economic damage by leaving the EU. So what did happen?

bbc.title: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, rebuking Trump's war with Iran
bbc.desc: The resolution is largely symbolic, but it adds to pressure on the White House to end the conflict once and for all.

bbc.title: Want to feel happier at work? Take a five-minute walk
bbc.desc: Sitting for prolonged periods is associated with health complications – but you can counteract the risks of a sedentary life.

bbc.title: Teen boy arrested on suspicion of murder after 14-year-old girl reported missing
bbc.desc: A body is found in the search for Lilly, 14, who was reported missing on Saturday.

bbc.title: Largest maternity review in NHS history to be published
bbc.desc: The review of Nottingham University Hospitals (NUH) NHS Trust is expected to detail how failings led to deaths and avoidable harm.
